Justin Bieber shared the Bieber family secret to living on Instagram.
Numbers one through ten emphasized words like worship, servanthood and generosity.
October 2025: Jack dresses as JB
Jack may have taken home the title for the best 2025 halloween costume.
The couple dressed Jack in Justin Bieber's "My World Tour" 2010s outfit. He was suited in white jeans and a purple sweatshirt with a white denim jacket over.
"Happy Halloween from JBB 🎃🎃🎃💜," Hailey Bieber wrote.
October 2025: Hailey reveals her favorite song on Justin's latest album
In an interview with BAZAAR, Hailey Bieber sat down to answer "burning questions." One of them being, which artist or album she was  obsessing over.
Justin Bieber released his album "Swag" in July 2025 and months later, Hailey Bieber was still hooked.
"Well, I think it goes without saying that 'Swag' is my album on repeat currently," Hailey Bieber said.
And as fans speculated, Hailey Bieber revealed her favorite track on Justin Bieber's July 2025 album.
"I love 'Daisies,' but I love the whole thing, so it’s hard for me to choose," she admitted.
November 2025: The Biebers dress as the Incredibles
For the Biebers, Halloween didn't stop in October.
Justin Bieber shared photos of him, Hailey and Jack posed in full "Incredibles" gear.
In the photos the power-couple is seen striking their best superhero poses and Hailey Bieber holds her real life version of baby Jack-Jack from the "Incredibles."
November 2025: Hailey debuts on the cover of GQ Men of the Year

The same year she sold her beauty brand Rhode in a deal worth $1 billion, Hailey Bieber starred in GQ’s Men of the Year 2025 edition. She opened up about balancing her public and private life as a family.
“We’re just taking it a day at a time,” she said.

“We both feel very protective of our son and I don’t think that’s ever going to change, but our life is our life and it is really public, so I think we’re just going to cross every bridge that we need to when we get there. But as of right now, I feel really comfortable about the way we are sharing things and not sharing things,” Hailey Bieber continued.

title: What Does Bad Bunny's 'Debí Tirar Más Fotos' Mean? Song Lyrics Translated
summary: Bad Bunny’s song “DtMF” topped charts and became a viral sensation following the release of his sixth studio album, “Debí Tirar Más Fotos.”
story: Bad Bunny’s song “DtMF” topped charts and became a viral sensation following the release of his sixth studio album, “Debí Tirar Más Fotos.” Both translate to “I Should’ve Taken More Photos.”
The song is about spending time with the people around you, documenting as much as possible before things change, and then acknowledging that they already have changed.

The title track also tackles themes of displacement of Puerto Ricans. In the song, he aches to collect memories with loved ones while also painting a picture of going out in Puerto Rico, with dominoes, drinks, family and friends.

This thread weaves throughout the “Debí Tirar Más Fotos” album, which honors Puerto Rican histories, incorporates various musical genres native to Puerto Rico and uplifts communities (while also providing beats you can dance your heart out to).
“DtMF” is nominated for song of the year and record of the year at the 2026 Grammys. The full album is also nominated for multiple awards, including album of the year.
With its sentimental tones, “DtMF” started a trend online where users add the song to nostalgic videos, looking back at heartfelt memories of loved ones, pets and experiences.

In January 2025, following the release of the album and the start of the “DtMF” trend, Bad Bunny posted a TikTok with the song playing and his face teary-eyed. The caption simply, “tik tok...,” referring to seeing all the emotional videos of people online using his audio.

The Puerto Rican singer, whose real name is Benito Antonio Martínez Ocasio, held a 30-day residency in “No Me Quiero Ir De Aquí,” which translates to “I Don’t Want to Leave Here,” in the capitol of the archipelago which boosted the economy, brought in tourism and celebrated the culture. Bad Bunny is also touring in South America and Europe.
During an with i-D magazine in September 2025, the singer spoke about his decision to not announce a tour in the U.S., partially due to the ongoing ICE raids in the country.
On top of his busy touring schedule, Bad Bunny is also the Apple Music Super Bowl LX Halftime Show performer.
Below, read the lyrics to "Debí Tirar Más Fotos," translated to English by TODAY.com.
'Debí Tirar Más Fotos' Lyrics Translated to English
Another beautiful sunset I see in San Juan
Enjoying all of those things that those that leave miss
Enjoying those kinds of nights that don’t happen anymore
That don’t happen anymore
But wanting to go back to the last time
That I looked in your eyes
And tell you the things I haven’t told you (you look like my crush, haha)
And take the picture that I didn’t take (Wow, you look so beautiful, let me take a photo)
Ey, my chest got scratched, I fell down
My heart, kicking me
Tell me baby, where you at?
To get there with RoRo, Julito, Krystal
Roy, Edgar, Seba, Óscar, Darnell y Big Jay, playing drums
Today we leave the street destroyed
It would be awesome that you touch my güiro
I see your name and I let out sighs
I don’t know if it’s fireworks or shots
My blanquita, my coke, my kilo I’m in PR, chillin’, but
[Chorus]
I should’ve taken more pictures when I had you
I should’ve given you more kisses and hugs the times that I could
I hope my people never move
And if I get drunk today, well, that they help me
I should’ve taken more pictures when I had you
I should’ve given you more kisses and hugs the times that I could
I hope my people never move

title: Who's in the Cast of Season 4 of 'The White Lotus'?
summary: Who is in Season 4 of The White Lotus? Here's a list of the cast including what they've been in before, from Helena Bonham Carter to Chris Messina.
story: A new group of wealthy guests have made their reservations for what is sure to be a dramatic French vacation when "The White Lotus" returns for its fourth season.
The upcoming season of the HBO hit will feature the cast at a luxury property in France, after previous seasons were set in opulent locations in Thailand, Italy and Hawaii.
We also now know who will make up the new cast on the show, which has rotated in almost entirely new groups of actors every season under writer, director and executive producer Mike White.

They will have a tough act to follow, as seven actors from season three (Parker Posey, Jason Isaacs, Aimee Lou Wood, Carrie Coon, Sam Rockwell, Walton Goggins, Natasha Rothwell) all received Primetime Emmy nominations.

The plotline and characters for Season Four have not yet been revealed, but here's what we know about who will be featured in the newest season of rich hotel guests behaving badly and the frazzled staff who have to deal with them.
This story will be updated as more information about the cast emerges.
Helena Bonham Carter

It's nice to imagine the two-time Oscar nominee and nine-time Golden Globe nominee out in the sunshine on the French Riviera, as the British actor is best known for gloomy and gothic roles in movies like "Fight Club," "Sweeney Todd," the "Harry Potter" films, "Mary Shelley's Frankenstein," and a host of Tim Burton films.

Carter, 54, has also been honored by critics for her dramatic work in "The Wings of the Dove" and "The King's Speech." While she is best known for her film career, she has also done plenty of television work. She appeared on the Netflix hit "The Crown" and most recently is part of the British miniseries "Agatha Christie's Seven Dials," which was released on Jan. 15 on Netflix.
Chris Messina

Messina, 51, is best known for his work as Dr. Daniel Castellano on the comedy series "The Mindy Project" with Mindy Kaling, and he also starred as a sinister presence on the USA Network show "The Sinner" in 2020.

The actor is also no stranger to HBO projects, as he appeared as a detective on the acclaimed series "Sharp Objects" with Amy Adams in 2018. He also was part of the HBO hit "Six Feet Under" in 2005 and the drama "The Newsroom" from 2012 to 2014.
Marissa Long
This is the television debut for the model and actor.

"I’ll try and make this short and sweet But I am absolutely raw right now!!!" she wrote on Instagram about landing the role. "God is so good — I am in the middle of so many feelings and I’m unbelievably honored to be joining this with such incredible people."
Steve Coogan
A longtime comic actor, the British star is a two-time Oscar nominee for his 2013 movie "Philomena" with Judi Dench.
He's also known for his hilarious role as a doomed director in the comedy "Tropic Thunder," as well as the hit "Night at the Museum" films.

Coogan, 60, also starred in the cult 2002 film "24 Hour Party People" about the music scene in Manchester, England, and has been a staple of British television over the years with his Alan Partridge character.

Coogan also drew acclaim for his "The Trip" series of travelogues for the BBC between 2010-2020. His voice may sound familiar to fans of the animated "Despicable Me" movies as well as "The Minions" and "The Secret Life of Pets."
Caleb Jonte Edwards
Much like Marissa Long, this is the first major television project for Edwards, an Australian actor whose only previous credit is the Australian crime drama "Black Snow."
Alexander Ludwig
Best known for his role as Björn Ironside on the History Channel series "Vikings" from 2014-2020, Ludwig, 33, also had a memorable role in "The Hunger Games."
He also has appeared as a character in two of the "Bad Boys" films starring Will Smith and Martin Lawrence.
AJ Michalka
Michalka, 34, is best known for her work as a singer in the duo Aly & AJ with her older sister, Aly Michalka.
She also has been acting in films and movies since 2002, starting as a child actor with appearances on daytime soap operas and films like "The Lovely Bones."
Michalka additionally had a regular role on the ABC sitcom "The Goldbergs." In 2024, she popped up as one of the hidden performers on Season 12 of "The Masked Singer."

title: Legendary Actor Catherine O'Hara Dies at 71
summary: Catherine O'Hara, a legendary comedic actor known for "Beetlejuice," "Schitt's Creek" and more, has died at 71.
story: Catherine O'Hara, a legendary comedic actor whose career spanned 1980s hits like "Beetlejuice" to the current Apple TV sensation "The Studio," has died at 71, her manager confirmed to NBC News.
O'Hara died at home Jan. 30 after a brief illness, according to her agency.

The Canadian actor played wacky sculptor Delia Deetz in the 1988 hit "Beetlejuice" and starred as Macaulay Culkin's mom in the iconic 1990 hit "Home Alone." O'Hara reprised her role as Deetz in the 2024 sequel "Beetlejuice, Beetlejuice."

She also famously portrayed Cookie Fleck, the wife of Eugene Levy's character who keeps running into former boyfriends, in the 2000 comedy classic "Best In Show" about a prestigious competition in the vein of the Westminster dog show.

O'Hara re-teamed with Levy on the CBC comedy "Schitt's Creek" and won an Emmy for her role as eccentric former soap opera actor Moira Rose. The actor also won a Golden Globe in 2021 for her performance on "Schitt's Creek," which came into prominence when it was picked up by Netflix.
With her portrayal of the Rose family matriarch, O'Hara had fans cracking up with her unplaceable accent.

"The accent was kind of up to me," she told TODAY's Willie Geist during Sunday Sitdown in 2024. "The accents, I should say. When people try to imitate that character, the mistake most people make is to be consistent."
O'Hara was a 10-time Emmy nominee who took home a pair of awards for her acting in "Schitt's Creek" in 2020 and for her writing on the comedy show "SCTV Network 90" in 1982.

O'Hara was nominated for two Emmys in 2025, including one for outstanding supporting actress in a comedy series for her performance in the Apple TV hit "The Studio" with Seth Rogen. For her role as ex studio head Patty, she was also up for a 2026 Golden Globe Award, which were presented on Jan. 11.
She also showed off her versatility by earning an Emmy nomination for her dramatic work in the HBO series "The Last of Us," in which she played the therapist of Pedro Pascal's character, Joel Miller.
In the wake of her death, Pascal posted a photo of him and O'Hara on set in tribute.
"Oh, genius to be near you. Eternally grateful.  There is less light in my world, this lucky world that had you, will keep you, always," Pascal wrote in the caption.

She also appeared in the sequel “Home Alone 2: Lost in New York” and a host of mockumentaries by “Best In Show” director Christopher Guest like “Waiting for Guffman,” “A Mighty Wind” and “For Your Consideration.”
O'Hara grew up in Toronto as the sixth of seven children in a home filled with laughter.
"It was demanded more than encouraged in our home," she said on TODAY. "When you have that many people at the table, you better have something at least funny to say if not interesting."
When she was a teen, her brother was in a theater troupe with future "Saturday Night Live" legend Gilda Radner, who would come to O'Hara's home for dinners.

"She had my parents improv-ing and doing bits, too, and playing improv games," O'Hara said of Radner, who died in 1989. "She let all of us see that regular people that you know could actually be in show business and actually have a job doing this and get paid for doing this."

Radner inspired O'Hara to join the Second City comedy troupe at 18 years old. She came to prominence as a performer on "SCTV," a Canadian sketch comedy show that also featured legends like Levy, John Candy, Martin Short, Harold Ramis and Rick Moranis.
O’Hara is survived by her husband, Bo Welch, and sons Matthew and Luke, as well as siblings Michael O’Hara, Mary Margaret O’Hara, Maureen Jolley, Marcus O‘Hara, Tom O’Hara and Patricia Wallice.
A private celebration of life will be held by her family.

title: Macaulay Culkin Reacts to Catherine O’Hara’s Death: ‘Mama. I Thought We Had Time’
summary: Macaulay Culkin is mourning the death of Catherine O’Hara, who played his mother in the “Home Alone,” as well as its sequel, “Home Alone 2: Los in New York.”
story: Macaulay Culkin is mourning the death of Catherine O’Hara, who played his mother in the 1990 smash comedy “Home Alone” and its 1992 sequel, “Home Alone 2: Lost in New York.”
O’Hara died at the age of 71, her manager confirmed to NBC News. A cause of death has not been indicated.
“Mama. I thought we had time. I wanted more,” Culkin wrote on Instagram Jan. 30. “I wanted to sit in a chair next to you. I heard you. But I had so much more to say. I love you. I’ll see you later.

The pair had crossed paths in the years after “Home Alone.” In 2023, O’Hara spoke when Culkin received a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame, complimenting his work playing a young child whose parents accidentally left him behind while on a family trip in the first film, a theme that would recur in the sequel.
“Thank you for including me, your fake mom who left you home alone not once, but twice, to share in this happy occasion. I’m so proud of you,” she said.

“I know you worked really hard. I know you did,” she continued. “But you made acting look like the most natural thing in the world to do. It really was as if we had ambushed the home of this real little boy named Kevin to make a movie and he just went along with it for the fun of it. It’s the dearest thing.”

She has also recalled that she has been mistaken for being Culkin’s real mother, including at the Walk of Fame ceremony when he was asked to take some photos with Culkin and actor Natasha Lyonne, who was also on hand.

“His mother was not able to make it at that time. But we’re all posing, posing, posing,” she told TODAY’s Willie Geist during a 2024 Sunday Sitdown. “And then — now Natasha, now so-and-so. ‘OK, now just you and the mother.’ And I walk away and he said, ‘Just with the mother.’
“Then he puts his arm around, he goes, ‘Oh, no, my mom wasn’t able to make it,’” she continued. “‘Well then, I’m honored to be here. Thank you.’”
The “Home Alone” role would stick with O’Hara throughout her career.
“A child came up to me, like, a little 8-year-old, in a mall,” she told Willie.
“’Are you Kevin’s mom?’ I said, ‘Well, yeah, I played Kevin’s mom in a movie, yes.’ ‘Why did you leave him?’ ‘Sorry. It was in the script.’”

title: Bruno Mars and Rosé's 'APT.' Is a Hit, But What Does the Song Mean? Lyrics Explained
summary: What does "APT" mean? A Korean expert explains the song's meaning.
story: "APT.," one the biggest hits of the year by Rosé and Bruno Mars certainly has the potential to win big at the Grammys on Feb. 1.
The song is up for three nominations: Song of the year, record of the year and best pop duo/group performance. Bruno Mars and Rosé are also among the night's performers.
The song, released in 2024, brings together two forces in the music world: Many time Grammy-winner Bruno Mars and Rosé, a member of the K-pop girl group Blackpink.
Jae-Ha Kim, a journalist who specializes in Korean culture, says the song's cross-cultural appeal is no surprise.

"The entire song is an earworm! It has a catchy beat, English lyrics that are easy to sing along with, and two giant pop stars. Rosé and Bruno Mars’ voices blend so well together and they exude fun in the accompanying music video, which is really refreshing in its simplicity. It was also helpful that both artists have huge fanbases that supported the song from the get-go, which made the song impossible for the general public to ignore," she told TODAY.com in a statement.
What does the K-pop song mean? Here's what to know.
What Does 'APT.' Mean?
According to Kim, the title of the song, and the recurring word in Korean that pops up throughout the song, is the word for apartment.
"But Koreans don’t say ‘apartment.’ They say apateu/아파트(pronounded ah-pah-teu). Apateu is a word Koreans have used for decades," Kim said.
Fans of K-dramas might have also been familiar with this word before the song came around.

"In the 1980s, having a modern apartment was a status symbol," Kim continued. "K-drama viewers may recall hearing the word in shows, where the characters talk about wanting to move out of their old house into a new apateu."
What Rosé Has Said About the Lyrics’ Meaning
The lyrics have a double meaning. "Apateu" is also a popular drinking game in Korea. "In Korea, it’s called the Apartment Game," Kim said.
During an appearance on “The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon," Rosé said the song was inspired by turning the legal drinking age in Korea and playing APT with Blackpink.

“I remember when I turned 20, my Blackpink — the girls in the group, they got like, all the alcohol we could get. We were like, ‘We’re gonna drink tonight,” she told Fallon. “We’d play drinking games and ‘APT’ was one of them.”
The word "apateu" is repeated during the drinking game.
She explained how she brought the concept to the studio in an interview with Vogue.
"'Basically, we were hanging out in the studio and I was like, 'How about I teach you guys some Korean drinking games,' and then I started doing it," she said.
Rosé said her team was "obsessed" with the game and she decided to write a song about it. She started to second-guess herself soon after.

“I remember going home kind of freaked out. I was like, ‘Is this OK that I’ve written a song about a drinking game? Is this too unserious?’ And so I got all of the producers and songwriters to delete the song off of their phones.”
After showing it around to a few people, she said everyone was "so obsessed" with the song that she decided to bring it back to life and finish it.

title: Margot Robbie and Jacob Elordi Reveal ‘Wuthering Heights’ Director Made Shrines of Them In Their Dressing Rooms
summary: Margot Robbie and Jacob Elordi have a dynamic relationship on and off the screen.
story: Margot Robbie and Jacob Elordi have a dynamic relationship on and off the screen.
The actors, who bonded over their Australian roots, co-star in the highly anticipated film “Wuthering Heights,” directed by Emerald Fennell.
Based on Emily Brontë's 1847 novel, the story is set in the late 18th and early 19th century and follows the heartbreaking and destructive romance between Heathcliff and Catherine Earnshaw.

Committed to the brooding storyline, Robbie, 35, and Elordi, 27, revealed to TODAY correspondent Kaylee Hartung that their director made sure they worked on cultivating their romance between their characters.

“At the studio, we both had a dressing room. Emerald went into our dressing rooms and made a shrine of each other in our dressing rooms,” Robbie shared, with Elordi calling the move “funny” and “fantastic.”

Robbie added, “It was so unhinged and so hilarious. And she put, like, locks of hair and pictures of us, candles and made a full shrine... Mine was a Jacob shrine. He had a Margot shrine. It was so funny.”
The “Barbie” star explained that Fennell “set the bar” for how intense they should take their character’s romance.
“And then we just kept trying to meet the bar,” she said.
The Oscar nominated stars’ press tour has been filled with praise for each other, including Robbie telling Fandango that she became “codependent” on Elordi during the filming.
“I love everyone so much and I’m always that person who’s so devastated when a job’s over and I never want it to end,” she said. “I think I developed that quite quickly with Jacob, too.”
Elordi said during that interview that they had ⁠⁠“a mutual obsession.”

“If you have the opportunity to share a film set with Margot Robbie, you’re going to make sure you’re within 5 to 10 meters at all times, watching how she drinks tea, how she eats her food. She’s an elite actor,” he added. ⁠
Speaking with Hartung, Robbie said she “can’t imagine” doing the film with anyone other than Elordi.

“It would’ve been really difficult to do this role and play this relationship with someone who didn’t approach work in a same way that I do and that I didn’t genuinely get along with,” she said. “We both work hard and we both love what we do.”
The adoration is mutual for Elordi, who shared that he’s been a fan of Robbie since her work in the Australian soap opera “Neighbours.” Robbie was only 17 when she made her debut on the drama.

“I wanted to act so I had a hit list of every working Australian actor on my wall so I could find their agents and things that they’d been in,” Elordi said, before adding that seeing Robbie in “The Wolf of Wall Street” was when he knew she became “a global phenomenon.” “You came out in that film and something changed in the world, in my world.”
Both now Oscar-nominated actors, Elordi most recently for his role in “Frankenstein,” they said they find it “surreal” how far they’ve come.
“It’s like a silly big dream, you know?” Elordi said.

“It’s just kind of crazy that we’re here now in the middle of Hollywood doing this big Hollywood film,” Robbie added. “He’s from Brisbane, and I’m from the Gold Coast. Growing up, Hollywood feels so far away and this unattainable thing that you’re going to make it to. It’s kinda surreal.”
“Wuthering Heights” arrives in theaters on Feb. 13.

title: Who Is Alex Warren's Wife? All About Kouvr Annon, the Woman Who Inspired 'Ordinary'
summary: Who is Alex Warren's wife, Kouvr Annon? About their love story including when they met, how she inspired Ordinary, who introduced them and where they are now.
story: Alex Warren sings that his world was “black and white” until he met one not-so-ordinary woman in the hit song “Ordinary.”
Warren married his wife, Kouvr Annon, in 2024, and their relationship played out on social media for years before then. The two were both part of Hype House, a collective of social media influencers.
Annon has been by Warren’s side as they've skyrocketed to fame over the past eight years, from when they were sleeping in cars in California to Warren’s 2026 Grammy nomination.
Here’s what to know about Annon and their relationship.
She’s Originally from Hawaii
Annon is originally from Hawaii and graduated in 2018, according to a TikTok she made with Warren outside her high school.
In another TikTok, Warren and Annon surprise her family after she was gone for three years.
They Met in 2018 Through a Mutual Friend
Warren said, in a 2025 interview with the BBC, they were introduced through a mutual friend and connected on Snapchat.
“We just clicked right away. I felt I could tell her everything, just after our first conversation,” he said.
Four months later, according to the publication, Anon left Hawaii and moved to California to be with Warren. At the time, he was sleeping in a car, and she joined him.
They Were Part of Hype House
The couple posted videos of their relationship online and rose to fame. Warren, in 2019, co-founded Hype House, a group of creators and influencers living together under one roof.
The couple left in 2022 and went on to pursue each of their independent dreams. For Warren, that was songwriting.
Both remain influencers with huge followings. Annon, 25, has 16.3 million followers and documents her life, including her new horse, hobbies like knitting and interior design.
She’s Open About Her Health and Fertility Issues
Annon is also open about her Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) and fertility difficulties on her page, as well as her desire to be a mom.
The Couple Married in 2024

Warren kept all of social media on edge for when he would pop the question because of his videos joking about engagement. One of the more popular videos being one where he fake proposed for three years, so Annon would fail to expect the real proposal.
Warren finally got down on one knee for the real proposal on Dec. 23, 2022.
“I’m marrying my best friend ❤️💍,” Annon wrote on Instagram.
The couple got married in a fairy tale garden on June 22, 2024, according to People.
"Getting married is extremely important to me," Warren told People. "When I met Kouvr and the way she made me feel, I never wanted to let go of that feeling."

The ceremony was filled with easter eggs from the couple's past.  The couple was officiated by the man who took them in when the couple was sleeping in a car. Warren's late parents were also saved a seat at the ceremony and symbolized in his suit sunflower lining, for his mother, and pelican print vest in honor of his father.
“It felt so important to us to acknowledge that they were unable to be there in person but that we included them in our special day,” Annon also told People.
She Inspired the Song ‘Ordinary’
What followed was the birth of Warren’s hit song "Ordinary,” released in 2025.

“This song is now number 1 everywhere and I can’t help but smile to know it’s about you,” Warren wrote on Instagram. “It also reminds me that we’ve been together this long and I’ve never seen you run full speed.”
Warren also spoke to the music publication HITS about how rare he thinks it is to find true love today.
"We wrote ‘Ordinary’ about how love is falling out, but we found something crazy,” he said.
In a TikTok, Warren playfully joked with Annon about how she didn't know the lyrics.
“I thought the lyrics said, ‘And I’ll my life I’ll do ya,’” she said in the video.
“I thought you were talking about getting freaky, and I was, like, ‘Damn, my mom listens to this,’” she added.
The real lyrics go, “Oh, my life, how do ya?/ Breathe and take my breath away?”
Warren is now up for best new artist at the 2026 Grammys.
“Married to the best new artist…” Annon’s Instagram bio reads.

title: Kylie Kelce Has a Coy Answer to Question About Travis and Taylor’s Wedding on TODAY
summary: Kylie Kelce said she doesn't know when Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift will get married, even when TODAY's Savannah Guthrie pressed her on the matter.
story: Will Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce get married in 2026? If Kylie Kelce knows, she’s not letting on.

Kelce, who’s married to Jason Kelce and is sister-in-law to Travis Kelce, did her best to stay mum when she was asked about a possible wedding on TODAY. Kelce appeared on TODAY as a partner to Dove and exclusively revealed its Super Bowl spot.
“Do we think there’s a wedding this year?” Savannah Guthrie asked her Jan. 30 on TODAY.
“Oh, I don’t know,” Kelce replied with a shrug.
“OK. Do you really not know?” Savannah then asked.
“I have no idea,” Kelce responded. Then she grinned and laughed.
“You’re not going to lie,” Savannah said, in a nod to the name of Kelce’s podcast.
“Yes,” Kelce said, prompting Savannah to laugh. “I’m not going to lie.”
“You’re not going to get yourself in trouble, either, I know,” Savannah said.
“Never,” Kelce said. “We’re so excited to celebrate, though. We can’t wait.”
Swift and Travis Kelce announced their engagement in August, sending Swifties into a tizzy. Since then, the couple have not revealed any specifics about their wedding.
Swift, however, did say Travis Kelce popped the question shortly after she appeared on the “New Heights” podcast he hosts with his brother.
“He was top secret about this,” she said during an October appearance on “The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon.”
“And I really appreciate that. But then afterward I kind of realized, he’s walking around sort of nervous about the podcast.

“He’s like, ‘My heart is racing. I just really want — I know how much this means to you. I really want this to be what you hoped it would be,’ because we’d been talking about doing the podcast and announcing the album (‘The Life of a Showgirl’). He’s like, ‘My heart’s racing,’ and I’ve never seen this dude nervous ever. He’s not a nervous person.”

title: What Is a Ward? Explaining Sophie's Parentage, Social Status in 'Bridgerton' Season 4
summary: Sophie is described as being a ward in "Bridgerton" Season 4. What does that mean? Who are her parents? Her social status in Season 4 explained.
story: "Bridgerton" Season 4 has a new romantic lead who holds a unique place in London society.

Sophie Baek, played by Yerin Ha, is first introduced as the lady in silver, a woman who captivates our hero, Benedict (Luke Thompson), at the annual Bridgerton masquerade ball. But after a charged private conversation, she dashes off like Cinderella. Later, viewers find out why: She's a maid.

It's more complicated than that. In Episode 2, a series of flashbacks reveal the complicated circumstances of Sophie's place in society — and why it leaves her with guard constantly up, Ha tells TODAY.com.

The flashbacks begin with a young Sophie being raised by the Earl of Penwood, whom she calls "father." In the first scene, she stands outside with the house's staff as they await the arrival of the new Lady Penwood, Araminta Gun (Katie Leung), and her two daughters from a previous marriage. Sophie expresses her excitement at meeting her "new mother," but she's quickly chided by a servant to not call Lord Penwood father in front of his new wife.
When Araminta is introduced to Sophie for the first time, the earl calls Sophie, "my ward," as Araminta looks at him in shock.
What Is a Ward?
Today, ward is a legal term that refers to a minor placed under the protection of a court or court-appointed guardian.
In the Regency era, the term would more generally refer to any child requiring the protection of a guardian rather than a parent.

By calling her his ward, the Earl of Penwood claims responsibility for Sophie without claiming her as his own. But the flashbacks show that the earl raised her like nobility, providing dance lessons and tutoring for Sophie to complete with Lady Penwood's daughters, Rosamund and Posey.
"I will always make sure you are taken care of," he promises her after she asked about the identity of her mother.
After his death, Lady Penwood takes a different approach to caring for Sophie.
As Sophie mourns her father at his gravesite, Araminta reveals the truth about Sophie's parents.
Who Are Sophie's Parents?

Sophie is indeed the daughter of the Earl of Penwood. But as an illegitimate child, meaning she was born to two people who were not married, she carries little social status, and in fact, her very existence invokes scandal.
After the earl's death, Araminta finally tells Sophie the truth about her mother.
Sophie's mother was a maid who became the earl's mistress.
"She died when you were very small with no title, no money, no dignity and no husband," Araminta says, before claiming that Sophie was not included in the earl's will.
Araminta then reveals she will "take care" of Sophie by having her work as a maid in the Penwood house.
"But I am the daughter of a nobleman," Sophie protests.
“Being a maid is the best you can hope for. It will make you invisible,” Araminta says. “You will be protected this way. As long as you do not allow yourself to dream of more than your station.”
How Does the TV Show Change Sophie's Backstory?
While her situation in life is tragic, it's even more bleak in the original book, Julia Quinn's "An Offer From a Gentleman."
"Everyone knew that Sophie Beckett was a bastard," reads the book's opening line.

In the book, the earl does provide shelter for Sophie, but not much else. The prologue reveals that they would only see each other "a few times a year" and that Sophie was well aware of her illegitimate status, calling the earl simply, "my lord," rather than father.
Showrunner Jess Brownell tells TODAY.com that the change was made to "humanize" the people who raised her.
"There's a bit more distance and coldness between the earl and Sophie in the book, and on screen, it felt too bleak to have her father not acknowledge her," Brownell says.
It also provided a "dramatic payoff" for Sophie to discover the truth of her status.
"It actually, for us, created a bigger dramatic payoff for Sophie to have believed, truly believed, that her father loved her, and then have her stepmother try to deny that reality," Brownell says.
"So that's something that really plays into Sophie's character and into her approach with Benedict, actually."

title: 'Bridgerton' Star Yerin Ha Unpacks Sophie's Reaction to Benedict's Offer
summary: Why does Sophie run away from Benedict's offer to be his mistress in Season 4, Part 1 of Bridgerton?
story: Warning: This article contains major spoilers for the ending of "Bridgerton" Season 4, Part 1.
"Bridgerton" Season 4 lived up to the title of the Julia Quinn book that inspired it in the final moments of Part 1.

Across the season's first four episodes, which hit Netflix Jan. 29, second son Benedict Bridgerton tries to hunt down the lady in silver, a woman he had a romantic first meeting with at his mother's masquerade ball. But in true Cinderella story fashion, she's right under his nose the entire time.

The lady in silver is actually a maid named Sophie Baek, played by newcomer Yerin Ha. She's the illegimate daughter of the late nobleman the Earl of Penwood, and while she was originally raised as his ward, after his death, she forced to serve his wife and stepdaughters.

But she's expelled from her home once Lady Araminta Gun finds out about her masquerade ball adventure, leading her to seek work out in the countryside. And through a series of coincidences, she ends up needing to find shelter alongside a wounded Benedict, who, of course, doesn't recognize her.

While he's still looking for the owner of the glove that was left behind by the lady in silver, he also finds himself drawn to Sophie as she stays by his side in the country, resulting in a sweet lakeside kiss.
In Episode 4, Sophie returns to London with him and is hired by his mother to work at Bridgerton House, further complicating their chemistry.

In the final moments of the Part 1, they run into each other on the stairs and give into a passionate, steamy kiss, set to an orchestral cover of Olivia Rodrigo's "Bad Idea Right?" — until Benedict asks a question that changes everything.
More about 'Bridgerton' Season 4

‘Bridgerton’ Season 4 Is a ‘Cinderella’ Re-Telling: All the Twists, References and Easter Eggs‘Bridgerton’ Season 4, Part 1, Ending: What This Character’s Surprise Reappearance Means for Francesca‘Bridgerton’ Showrunner on How Season 4 Will Embrace Benedict’s Sexuality
‘Bridgerton’ Season 4 Is a ‘Cinderella’ Re-Telling: All the Twists, References and Easter Eggs
‘Bridgerton’ Season 4, Part 1, Ending: What This Character’s Surprise Reappearance Means for Francesca
‘Bridgerton’ Showrunner on How Season 4 Will Embrace Benedict’s Sexuality
What Did Benedict Ask Sophie?
At the end of "Bridgerton" Season 4, Part 1, Benedict and Sophie give into their attraction after trying to stave it off once Sophie became an employee of the Bridgerton family.
After a passionate kiss, Benedict starts to confess his feelings.

"If you wish me to leave, I will, but the truth is, I stay away because you consume me," he tells her. "My eyes search for you in every room I enter. My heart beats when you are near. The reality of you has become more tantalizing than any fantasy ever could be, and one I cannot live without."
Sophie appears moved, and whispers Benedict's name in response.
"I meant it when I said you deserve better, and I am determined to give it to you and more," he continues.
"Sophie ... be my mistress," he concludes.
The music abruptly stops, and Sophie looks crestfallen by the titular "Offer from a Gentleman," which doubles as the name of the episode. She runs off, wordless.
Why Is Sophie So Hurt by Benedict's Offer?
While Sophie says nothing in response to Benedict's proposition, her face says it all.
Yerin Ha, who plays Sophie, tells TODAY.com that in that moment, her character "feels like she's let her guard down too much, and it's a reminder of exactly why she has it up."

Ha says the moment takes Sophie back to her childhood, when she was forced from a life of comfort and love with her father into that of servitude. After her father's death, Araminta tells Sophie at the earl's grave that she was not included in his will.
"My father loved me," Sophie says.
Araminta then tells her that Sophie's mother was a maid who "let herself become a mistress" and died without a husband, dignity or a title.
"Do not worry. I will take care of you now. I will feed you, clothe you, and keep a roof over your head. You will work as a maid," Araminta says.
Sophie protests that she is the daughter of a nobleman, before Araminta reminds her that she is illegitimate.
"Being a maid is the best you can hope for. It will make you invisible," Araminta says. "You will be protected this way. As long as you do not allow yourself to dream of more than your station."
Benedict professing his affection but asking her only to be his mistress stirs up the lingering hurt Sophie feels at being reduced to a maid, Ha explains.
"It's that mantra that Araminta always tells her, 'Know your place.' And I think that kind of kicks into gear," Ha says.
But even if Benedict did propose marriage, as would be expected for a noblewoman, Ha argues her character would still feel disappointed.

"Even if he did say, 'Be my wife,' I think almost in a way, she would maybe react the same, because it’s like, how do people from two different worlds coexist together when society doesn’t want them to?" she says.
"So yeah, I think that was what was going through my mind," Ha adds. "And just how passionately he delivered that."
"And how hot the staircase was," Thompson chimes in of filming the (literally) steamy scene.
"They built this new set and it was 15 stories high but there was like loads of candles and obviously all the heat rises, so it was boiling, and you nearly caught fire," Thompson says.
"My hair almost caught fire," Ha confirms with a laugh.
"That's what she was thinking," Thompson concludes.
"Bridgerton" Season 4 will conclude with four more episodes dropping on Netflix Feb. 26.

title: 'Bridgerton' Season 4, Part 1, Ending: What This Character's Surprise Reappearance Means for Francesca
summary: What Michaela's return to "Bridgerton" Season 4 means for Francesca, according to showrunner Jess Brownell.
story: Warning: This article contains major spoilers for the ending of "Bridgerton" Season 4, Part 1, and the sixth "Bridgerton" novel, "When He Was Wicked."
Francesca Bridgerton is quickly learning that marital bliss can bring its own set of complications.

The third-eldest Bridgerton daughter, played by Hannah Dodd starting in Season 3, married John Stirling (Victor Alli) at the end of last season. Their relationship was defined by a mutual love of music and finding comfort in quiet.

But the end of the season shook things up after Francesca seemed disappointed by her first kiss with John. And when she met his cousin, Michaela (Masali Baduza), she was taken aback, stumbling over her words as she struggled to introduce herself.

While a brief moment, fans of the original Julia Quinn books know why that was significant — Francesca's book, "When He Was Wicked," follows not her romance with John, but her love story with his cousin, Michael Stirling, after John's untimely death.

The TV show adaptation is seemingly setting up a queer romance for Francesca down the line, but showrunner Jess Brownell confirms Francesca, like the other Bridgerton siblings, will get her own moment in the spotlight.

"Every Bridgerton will get a solo season. We will not combine siblings, ever. That is not in the cards," Brownell tells TODAY.com. (At the Season 4 premiere, she confirmed that the already-renewed Seasons 5 and 6 will follow Eloise and Francesca, but did not specify which character will be featured in which season.)
So first, Season 4 picks up with how things are going in her and John's marriage.
What's Going On Between Francesca and John in 'Bridgerton' Season 4?
During the time jump between seasons, Francesca and John had been living in Scotland at his ancestral home, Kilmartin Castle, with Eloise and Michaela.

Season 4 opens with their return to London, and in Part 1 it's revealed that Francesca has been struggling with their intimate relationship. In Episode 3, viewers learn she hasn't been "achieving her pinnacle," after she and John discuss why they haven't conceived a child yet.
Dodd tells TODAY.com Francesca and John's love story shows that a "relationship is work, and you have to continue to work at it every single day."

"'Bridgerton' is amazing for these gorgeous, massive love stories, but every single day there's still going to be work to do, and you have to choose to be in love and stay in love every single day," she says. "So it's really lovely to see these two people who met not that long ago working on it and having the bravery and the courage to discuss their needs and intimate needs as well."

Francesca, left confused by their conversation, goes on to ask both her sister-in-law Penelope and her mother about what exactly a "pinnacle" is, highlighting a sexual knowledge "imbalance" between men and women during the time period, Dodd says.

"Men are allowed to have this experience and knowledge surrounding this part of a relationship, and women are just sent in so innocent and with no understanding," Dodd says. "So it's really hard to communicate your wants and needs when you don't actually have a basic understanding. It was a really fun storyline that I think was comedic and funny and really fun, but also really highlights this imbalance within society."
By Episode 4, they have a candid conversation about their intimate relationship after Francesca fakes an orgasm.
"I hope you know that you are just right as you are. We are just right as we are right now," John tells her. "And we have all the time in the world for the rest."
What Happened Between Francesca and Michaela?
As for her visceral reaction to meeting Michaela, John’s cousin isn’t really mentioned in Season 4, though the show alludes to the fact that they spent time together while in Scotland.
In Episode 1, John suggests saying in London for the season, to which Francesca asks, “What about your ... cousin?”

“I noticed the two of you kept your distance from one another this summer,” John responds. “Which is understandable. We are newlyweds and yet we began our married life in a home that Michaela considers hers as much as ours.”
After Part 1, we aren't sure what — if anything — happened between Francesca and her husband's cousin.
But the final moments of Season 4, Part 1 reestablish their tension, with Michaela making a surprise return to London that again leaves Francesca speechless.
"I do hope you've missed me," Michaela says after stepping off her coach.
"What a wonderful surprise," Francesca finally stammers out.
"Is it? You look as though you've seen a ghost," Michaela responds.
Dodd tells TODAY.com that Michaela "stirs something up" in Francesca.
"Francesca lives her life in quite a regimented way, and she's a rule follower, and she puts herself in these boxes," Dodd says. "Michaela is the complete opposite of that."
Michaela's approach to life, plus her close relationship with John, is overall "quite confronting" to Francesca, Dodd says.
As for what may have happened in Scotland?
"You find more and more information out as the season goes on," Dodd says.
Brownell adds that they waited to bring Michaela back until halfway through the season to first get Francesca and John to a "comfortable place."
"She’s a chaotic character in a lot of ways, and I think she is going to challenge John and Francesca’s perfectly routine life," Brownell says.

title: What Is the True Story Behind 'Miracle: The Boys of '80'?
summary: What's the true story of the Miracle on Ice? The hockey team in the 1980 Olympics upset spoke to TODAY about the true story behind the Netflix documentary.
story: Nearly 50 years later, it's still a moment that can bring Americans to tears.

The "Miracle on Ice" remains one of the most iconic and patriotic moments in sports history, a landmark win amid the Cold War with the Soviet Union and one of the biggest upsets of all time in any sport.

The magical moment when the U.S. men's ice hockey team defeated the Soviet Union in the 1980 Olympics is being celebrated for a new generation with the Netflix documentary, "Miracle: The Boys of '80," that begins streaming on Jan. 30.
The documentary promises new footage and reminiscences from the major figures.
Here's what to know about the story.
What Is the 'Miracle on Ice'?

An unheralded group of college players primarily from Boston University and the University of Minnesota comprised the 1980 U.S. Olympic hockey team at the Winter Games on Team USA's home ice in Lake Placid, N.Y.

The Americans faced off against a dominant Russia squad that had won three straight Olympic gold medals and had not lost an Olympic hockey game in 12 years. They had won gold at five of the previous six Olympics.

Team USA was a massive underdog using unheralded players in their early 20s against a veteran Soviet team that featured a host of legendary players, including Vladislav Tretiak, considered the best goalie in the world at the time.
Thirteen days before they faced off in the Olympic medal round, Russia had crushed the U.S. 10-3 in an exhibition game.
However, the Americans pulled off an upset for the ages, shocking the Soviet Union, 4-3, as 34 million people watched at home.

Captain Mike Eruzione immortalized himself in hockey history when he scored the goal that made it 4-3 in the third period with 10 minutes left in the game. Goalie Jim Craig then stonewalled the Soviet Union the rest of the way to help seal the victory in a game where he finished with 36 saves.

"My first reaction was we had the lead and there was still 10 minutes left to play in the game," Eruzione told Craig Melvin on TODAY on Jan. 28. "I had an opportunity to help and it turned out to be the game-winner, and I guess I look back on it and take great pride in knowing I was able to do that."

Standout forward Buzz Schneider made it clear to the Russians early on that this was a different U.S. team when he scored to tie the game 1-1 in the first period. Mark Johnson also was a star of the game with two goals.
"We thought that if we kept the game close, we had an opportunity to hang with them," Schneider said on TODAY. "If we could until the end, maybe we had a shot to win."

The game ended with one of the most iconic sports broadcasting calls of all time. Play-by-play announcer Al Michaels, who is now an NFL broadcaster for Amazon, exclaimed, "Do you believe in miracles? Yes!"
The game has since been immortalized in the 2004 movie "Miracle" with Kurt Russell, Patricia Clarkson and Noah Emmerich.
Who was the Coach of the U.S. Team?
Herb Brooks became a hockey legend for his work in assembling the underdog team that shocked the world.
Played by Kurt Russell in "Miracle," Brooks pushed the players to the brink mentally and physically during training, as they bonded over withstanding his demanding  personality and regimen.
Many of the players entered training camp as fierce rivals from their college teams in Minnesota and Boston but found common ground thanks to Brooks' tactics.
His son and daughter appear in the Netflix documentary and describe their father's hard-nosed approach, which his players echoed on their appearance on TODAY.

"Not everybody could play for him," forward Rob McClanahan said. "He was a very demanding coach, but he's somebody I would play for any day. If you asked me to put my skates on tomorrow, I'd do it because he got his players to play at the highest level at the most important times."

Brooks was inducted into the U.S. Hockey Hall of Fame in 1990. He also coached the U.S. Olympic hockey team to silver in 2002 in Salt Lake City, and coached the New York Rangers, New Jersey Devils and Pittsburgh Penguins in the NHL.
The legendary coach was killed in a car accident at 66 in 2003 when he was working for the Penguins.
Did the U.S. Win the Olympic Gold Medal by Beating Russia?
The shocking win over the Soviet Union did not clinch the gold medal for Team USA.

They actually had to win one more game against a tough Finland team. The U.S. scored three unanswered goals in the third period to rally for a 4-2 win and bring home their first gold medal in ice hockey since the 1960 Olympics.
What Do the 'Miracle on Ice' Team Members Say About It Now?
In the new Netflix documentary, the players make a return to the rink in Lake Placid where the legendary upset took place.
"Whenever we go into that arena, it's memories," star forward Rob McClanahan said on TODAY. "They have our names up on the banners on the stands. It's a great place. Great memories."

"I think it's great because you look at the legacy of our team and all my teammates kind of did, and how each generation has been inspired by that," goalie Jim Craig said on TODAY. "So you walk in, and some dreams start there and some dreams end there, so it was really an awesome time to be back."
Eruzione also reflected on how much that single moment altered the trajectory of his life.

"It's a pretty good thing to have your life defined by," he said. "I've always said an athletic event clearly changed our lives, changed my life, but it didn't change who you are as a person. So I just take great pride and respect in knowing that my teammates and I were part of something so special."

"We had idea the world was watching the way they were," he continued. "To have people come up to me to this day, sometimes they'll cry, and they'll say, 'I remember where I was when we won,' and I always go, 'I didn't know you were on the team.' But that's what that moment was. We could use a 1980 now.'"
Has the U.S. Men's Hockey Team Won Olympic Gold Since the 'Miracle on Ice'?
No, the incredible victory also marked the last time Team USA has finished atop the medal stand in Olympic men's ice hockey.
The only Olympic gold medals the U.S. has won in the sport came in 1960 and 1980. They won the silver in 2002 and 2010.

The upcoming Winter Olympics in Milan looks like a golden opportunity to end the drought. The star-studded U.S. team featuring a host of NHL players is expected to be a primary challenger to their nemesis, Canada, for gold in Italy.
They also have gotten a pep talk from Eruzione himself.

"I told the players, I got to know almost all of them, I said, 'Do our team a favor and win the gold medal so people will leave us alone in the truck. It's your time to carry the torch,'" he joked. "I think this team can do it, and I think they're going to win it."

title: ‘The Traitors’ Season 4, Episode 7 Recap: Who Was Banished and Who Was Murdered?
summary: After last week’s teaser featuring host Alan Cumming inviting the players to his murderous banquet, fans were eagerly anticipating how the episode would unfold. Find out what happened in Episode 7.
story: After last week’s teaser featuring host Alan Cumming inviting the players to his murderous banquet, fans were eagerly anticipating how the episode would unfold. But did it live up to the hype?

The seventh episode of Season 4, titled “The Black Banquet,” arrived on Peacock Jan. 29 and immediately welcomed the players and the viewers to the dinner party Cumming mentioned in the previous episode. To recap, Cumming tasked the Traitors — “The Real Housewives of Beverly Hills” alum Lisa Rinna former “The Real Housewives of Potomac” star Candiace Dillard Bassett and “Love Island USA” Season 6’s Rob Rausch — with committing their next murder in plain sight. The host gifted them an amulet that one of them had to wear. If one of the players touched the cursed accessory, they would be murdered that night.
Rinna stepped up and decided she would be the one to complete the task. So was she successful? Find out below in a recap of the latest episode, which also breaks down a betrayal at the Round Table.
The Banquet Begins

All the players arrive dressed to the nines for Cumming’s banquet. But as he mentioned, the Traitors are “dressed to kill.” “Survivor” winner Yamil “Yam Yam” Arocho is already on high alert and says he will avoid taking drinks or food from anyone, expecting a twist is on the way.

Meanwhile, Rinna is looking forward to pulling off the challenging task. “This is like the Super Bowl of murders,” she says in a voiceover, adding that she wants the event to be “the most iconic banquet in ‘Traitor’’s history.” She reveals that her shortlist for murder includes Arocho, fellow “Survivor” winner Natalie Anderson and “Top Chef” host Kristen Kish.

As the players fix their plates and notice different items around the room, like a set of tarot cards with their faces, many suspect that the Traitors are up to something. Viewers then learn in a confessional from Rinna that the deed is done and she has already chosen her victim.

After the group goes around the table and says how much they have enjoyed getting to know one other, there is a forceful knock at the door and Cumming storms in with two masked, hooded figures. He takes a seat at the table and tells the players “the legend of the black dinner.” He explains that in 1440 Scottish King James II invited a noble clan to a castle. Unbeknownst to them, it was a trap. “Well, I’m afraid my dear players that tonight is a little case of history repeating,” he says.

Cumming shares that the cursed player could be saved, before his guards present the players with two vials filled with an antidote. The host explains that if the cursed player drinks the antidote, then he or she will be saved. He gives them a short amount of time to decide which two players think they are the most vulnerable. Ultimately, a vocal Anderson drinks the antidote and Olympic figure skater Johnny Weir hands Kish the other vial.

But, the group chose incorrectly as Arocho is revealed to be the cursed player. As he is dragged away from the table by the guards, he uses his final words to call out Rinna, claiming a kiss from her marked him for death. She attempts to defend herself by claiming she welcomed multiple players with air kisses.
The Power of the Dagger Is Introduced

All three Traitors arrive first to breakfast the next morning. Rinna calls out Rausch for voting for her last episode but he pushes back, saying he didn’t want to blow his cover. Rinna asks both Rausch and Dillard Bassett to defend her following Arocho’s accusation.

After breakfast, Dillard Bassett joins a few other players in suspecting Anderson is a Traitor following her behavior at the banquet. But, Rausch continues to turn on his fellow Traitors and tells a group of players that Rinna looks guilty. He says that Arocho had no reason to lie during his final minutes.

At the mission, the players have to race up a steep hill, collecting gold along the way, to reach a monument. The players who collect the most gold will then compete for the dagger, which gives one player the power of two votes at a Round Table. The power can only be used once in the next four Round Tables.

Rausch says in his confessional that he wants the dagger so he can gain more power. As he makes his way up the hill, he reiterates to “The Bachelor” star Colton Underwood that Rinna cannot be trusted.

The players finish the mission and add $17,800 to the prize pot. The top six players who collected the most gold are Underwood, Rausch, “One Tree Hill” alum Stephen Colletti, Anderson, “Dancing With the Stars” pro Mark Ballas and singer Eric Nam.

Rausch collected the most gold so he has an advantage in the next round. The other five remaining players have to flip over a card which will reveal if they have the dagger. Rausch then will question all of them to determine who is lying. If he guesses correctly, he takes the card with the dagger. Rausch decides Anderson is being dishonest and he is correct. However, only Rausch learns that he won the dagger.
When they return to the castle, Rausch shares with the other five that he has the dagger. He tells them to keep it a secret between the six of them.
An OG Traitor Is Banished

During the Round Table discussion, Rinna and Weir bring up points about Anderson. But Underwood comes after Rinna, again, and Rausch backs him. Rausch says the group shouldn’t focus on their emotions and that logically it doesn’t make sense for Anderson to put a target on her back during the banquet.

The group votes and a majority write Rinna’s name, including Rausch. However, Dillard Bassett decides to throw some suspicion his way and votes for the “Love Island USA” star, explaining that she couldn’t vote for Rinna or Anderson.

After all the votes are revealed, a tearful Rinna stands on the Circle of Truth and says, “First of all, I have absolutely loved being here with all of you. I never thought I could do this. I never thought I could last this long. What I’m going to tell you tonight is, I am the most iconic, legendary, Traitor!”

In her final confessional, Rinna says through tears, “Rob, damn you. Damn you.” She adds, “You know, the end of ‘Housewives’ was very difficult for me. It was just so nice to be able to be me. I’m just so grateful for the entire experience. I really am. Thank you.”
Traitors’ Trust Is Broken
Back in the castle, the fractured trust between the Traitors is irreparable. Rausch is mad at Dillard Bassett for writing his name and the feeling is mutual.

“Ladies and gentlemen, we have a snake on our hands,” Dillard Bassett says in her confessional. “This was buffoonery of the most epic proportions. He is willing to betray the people that helped him. He made certain, vicious choices that will have dire consequences for him.”

As the episode ends, Underwood shifts his focus to Colletti but Weir is convinced “The Bachelor” star is the real Traitor. Weir is also suspicious of Rausch, who tells a group that it is possible the turret was full of “Housewives” stars, pointing fingers at Dillard Bassett and “The Real Housewives of New York City” alum Dorinda Medley.

Before meeting in the turret, Dillard Bassett tells the cameras, “Rob made a decision to disband the Traitors. The Christian woman in me is just clutching her pearls. I’m aghast. But the bad b---- that is Candiace is ready to play a m----------- game. I have to take the snake down and cut him off at the head.”
The episode concludes with Dillard Bassett and Rausch meeting in the turret. The fallout between the Traitors will continue with Episode 8, out Feb. 5.

title: Chappell Roan Says Her Grammy-Nominated Song 'The Subway' Was Inspired by Someone She Was 'In Love With'
summary: What do the lyrics of Chappell Roan's The Subway mean? HEre's what Chappell Roan said about the real relationship that inspired the song.
story: The lyrics to Chappell Roan's hit song "The Subway" will take you on a ride.
Released in July 2025, “The Subway” is nominated for two 2026 Grammys in the categories of best pop solo performance and record of the year.

Roan first teased “The Subway” at a live concert at the 2024 Governors Ball music festival while dressed as a New York taxi with her skin painted green to represent the Statue of Liberty. She released the official recorded version of the song over a year later.

Roan posted a lengthy caption on Instagram about the journey to the song’s release. She said in the year after its first performance, she “ripped (her) hair out trying to figure out the puzzle of how this song should feel musically and visually and emotionally.”
"Thank you for sticking it out for a whole year. It was worth it to make sure everything was absolutely right," she wrote.
The Meaning Behind the Song Lyrics of 'The Subway'

A melancholy tune, "The Subway" is about a girl reminiscing about a past relationship. Everywhere she goes, she's reminded of her lover — whether in green hair on the subway or someone wearing her perfume at a party.
The feelings linger and she's hoping, eventually, they'll fade: "But I’m still counting down all of the days/‘Til you’re just another girl on the subway
Roan said the song was inspired by "someone she was deeply in love with."

"It was actually about me hiding in Los Angeles from someone I was deeply in love with, but we weren't on bad terms. Like it was just trying to avoid the coffee shops we went to and parties," she told Apple Music in August 2025.

One of the lyrics went particularly viral: A mention of Saskatchewan. The verse explains that if Roan can't get over her relationship, she'll have to move out of New York — and chooses the province in Western Canada as her destination.
Roan said she simply chose it because it rhymed.
"We needed something 'on,'" Roan told Apple Music. "I've never been there, but I promise I will perform there one day. I also just love that the capital is called Regina."
Around halfway through the tune, the verse "she's got a way" is sung repeatedly — with minor, but important, variations. The lyrics go from "she's got a way" to "she got away."
In the music video, Roan chases a girl with green hair around New York. She keeps getting tangled in her own long hair, perhaps symbolizing being caught in her feelings.
Read the Lyrics to 'The Subway' in Full:
I saw your green hair, beauty mark next to your mouth
There, on the subway, I nearly had a breakdown
A few weeks later, somebody wore your perfume
It almost killed me, I had to leave the room
It's just another day and it's not over (Ah)
'Til it's over, it's never over
It's just another day and it's not over (Ah)
'Til it's over, it's never over
'Til I don't look for you on the staircase
Or wish you thought that we were still soulmates
But I'm still counting down all of the days
'Til you're just another girl on the subway
Made you the villain, evil for just moving on
I see your shadow, I see it even with the lights off
I made a promise if in four months, this feeling ain't gone
Well, f--k this city, I'm moving to Saskatchewan
It's just another day and it's not over (Ah)
'Til it's over, oh, 'til it's over
It's just another day and it's not over (Ah)
'Til it's over, it's never over
'Til I can break routine during foreplay
And trust myself that I won't say your name
But I'm still counting down all of the days
'Til you're just another girl on the subway
She's got, she's got a way
She's got a way, she's got a way
And she got, she got away
She got away, she got away
And she's got, she's got a way
She's got a way, she's got a way
And she got, she got away
She got away, she got away
(She's got, she's got a way
She's got a way, she's got a way)
She's got a way (She got, she got away
She got away, she got away)
She's got, she's got a way
She's got a way, she's got a way
And she got, she got away
She got away, she got away

title: Odessa A'Zion Responds to Fan Backlash, Drops Out of a Movie: 'I'm Out'
summary: "Marty Supreme" star Odessa A’zion dropped out of a movie Deep Cuts after backlash over her casting.
story: "Marty Supreme" star Odessa A’zion thanked fans for bringing a controversy to her attention and announced she is "out" on accepting a role that had sparked an online backlash.

A’zion, 25, was touted to be among the cast for the upcoming movie "Deep Cuts" in multiple reports only two days earlier. She shared in her Instagram Stories on Jan. 28 that she is no longer playing the role.
Here's what to know about the situation.
What Was the Controversy About Odessa A'zion's Role?
The upcoming A24 film "Deep Cuts" from director Sean Durkins is based on a 2025 novel by Holly Brickley of the same name.

A'zion was slated to play a character named Zoe Gutierrez, who is half Jewish and half Mexican in the novel. A'zion does not have Mexican heritage, which led some fans online to say the role was being whitewashed by giving it to a non-Hispanic actor.
A'zion noted in her Instagram Stories that she had never read the book, so she wasn't aware of the character's heritage.
The film also includes "Wake Up Dead Man" star Cailee Spaeny as music critic Percy Marks, and "Outer Banks" star Drew Starkey as songwriter Joe Morrow, the romantic interest of Spaeny's character.
What Did Odessa A'zion Say About Leaving the Film?

A'zion, who is the daughter of "Better Things" star Pamela Adlon and German director Felix Adlon, posted a series of messages on her Instagram stories after announcing she was withdrawing from the film.
"Guys!! I am with ALL of you and I am NOT doing this movie,” she initially posted. “F--- that. I’m OUT.”
A'zion then explained her decision.

"THANK YOU guys for bringing this to my attention. I AGREE WITH EVERY SINGLE ONE OF YOU!" she wrote. "This is why I love you guys. I’m so sorry that this happened. It is SO important for me to let you in on how it all went down: I went in for Percy, but was offered Zoe instead and instantly said yes! I’m so pissed y’all, I hadn’t read the book and should have paid more attention to all aspects of Zoe before accepting…and now that I know what I know???"
The "Fam" and "I Love LA" star then concluded by saying she looks forward to seeing who gets the role.

“I’d never take a role from someone else that’s meant to do it," she wrote. "That SHOULD do it! That’s not me. There are a plentitude of people more than capable of playing this role and I am NOT one of them. I can’t wait to see who it ends up being.”

title: ‘Bridgerton’ Showrunner on How Season 4 Will Embrace Benedict’s Sexuality
summary: Showrunner Jess Brownell discusses how Benedict Bridgerton's sexuality will continue to be embraced in Season 4.
story: Warning: This story contains spoilers for "Bridgerton" Season 4, Part 1.
Benedict Bridgerton's love life hasn't been the primary focus on the romantic Regency era show until now, but viewers have been paying close attention.

Since his introduction in Season 1, Luke Thompson's character has been a fan favorite, and his recurring pursuit of life outside of the traditional Ton society had many speculating Benedict may be queer. But it wasn't until Season 3, when he embarked on a relationship with Tilley Arnold and her friend Paul, that his sexuality was confirmed.

Thompson previously told Bustle that the best way to describe his character's sexuality in modern terms is pansexual, or "being attracted to the way that someone thinks and feels, regardless of gender," he explained.
"That’s a word that could be used. But what’s refreshing about it, certainly in the way that it’s being discovered at the moment, is that there is a sense of label-lessness about it," he said.

While he shares a brief kiss with a man in Episode 1 of Season 4, much of the season follows his love story with a woman named Sophie. But in Part 2, which is set to hit Netflix on Feb. 26,  showrunner Jess Brownell confirms his identity will continue to be explored.

"It's important for me that for any person who is queer — whether they're pansexual, bisexual, fluid, however they describe themselves — that even if that person ends up in a heterosexual presenting relationship, they are still queer," Brownell tells TODAY.com.

"It was really important to me and to the writers room to find ways to make it clear that Benedict still owns that part of himself," she adds. "So that is something that will be touched on here and in other seasons."
When discussing his character's pansexuality, Thompson said at the time that Benedict specifically approaches romance "in a spirit of curiosity."
That curiosity is also what's driving his pursuit of Sophie, or the “lady in silver,” after he meets the mystery woman at a masquerade ball in Episode 1.

“That life of dabbling is growing thin,” Brownell says of Benedict in Season 4. “And the moment he meets Sophie and sees the childlike wonder on her face as she takes in this ball, I think it is just this instant feeling of, ‘That is the feeling I’m missing in my life.’ Benedict has done it all and seen it all, and he’s growing jaded.
“So to meet someone who everything in society is new and exciting, is really exciting for him,” she adds.

title: Who Is Performing at the 2026 Grammys?
summary: Who is performing at the 2026 Grammys? Here's a list of everyone performing from Sabrina Carpenter to Pharrell Williams to best new artists like Olivia Dean.
story: Who is performing at the 2026 Grammys? The entire best new artist category, that's who.
As the Feb. 1 award ceremony approaches, the Recording Academy has been releasing the names of the performers viewers can expect to see take the stage at Crypto.com Arena in Los Angeles.

So far, the Recording Academy confirmed that all eight best new artist nominees will perform as part of a special segment. This will be the second year in a row that every artist in the category will sing.

So, who are all the best new artist nominees this year, and what other major artists have also been revealed as performers? Read on to learn everything you need to know about the 2026 Grammys performances, below.
Lady Gaga

Lady Gaga is no stranger to the Grammy stage. She will make a comeback this year for her 9th Grammy performance. The singer is heading into the award show with seven nominations from the Recording Academy. Gaga's latest album, "Mayhem," earned recognition for best pop vocal album and album of the year.

The album's hit song "Abracadabra" is nominated for record of the year, song of the year, and best dance pop of the year. It doesn't stop there — Gaga is nominated for best pop solo performance for her song "Disease" and best traditional pop vocal album for the "Joker" movie album "Harlequin."
Justin Bieber

Justin Bieber is making his grand return to the Grammy Awards after four years away. The Recording Academy revealed Jan. 28 that the pop star, who is nominated for four Grammys at this year's ceremony, is set to perform. Bieber was recognized for his latest album, Swag, which was released in July 2025. He is up for album of the year, best pop solo performance for his song "Daisies," best pop vocal album and best R&B performance for "Yukon."
Lauryn Hill, Reba McEntire and Post Malone

The Recording Academy has tapped multiple artists to pay tribute to the musicians who died last year during the In Memoriam segment of the ceremony. Reba McEntire will share the stage with Brandy Clark and Lukas Nelson to honor multiple musical icons. Lauryn Hill will also perform a tribute to D'Angelo and Robert Flack. To celebrate Ozzy Osbourne's legacy, Post Malone, Andrew Watt, Chad Smith, Duff McKagan and Slash will perform together.
Sabrina Carpenter

After gracing the stage last year to perform a medley of her hits “Espresso” and “Please Please Please,” Carpenter is back to sing songs from latest album, “Man’s Best Friend.” The Recording Academy announced Carpenter as the first performer this year.
Carpenter is already a two-time Grammy winner after receiving golden gramophones in 2025 for best pop vocal album for “Short n’ Sweet” and best pop solo performance for “Espresso.”

When the 2026 Grammy nominations were revealed in November, Carpenter received six nods in categories like album of the year, song of the year and record of the year. Fans will have to tune in on Feb. 1 to find out if she adds more Grammy wins to her resume.
Clipse and Pharrell Williams
Clipse, a hip-hop duo nominated for five Grammys this year, will team up with Pharrell Williams for an epic performance.

Williams, a 13-time Grammy winner, received four nods. Clipse — who consists of Gene “Malice” and Terrence “Pusha T” Thornton — and Williams collaborated on “Let God Sort Em Out,” which is nominated for album of the year and best rap album.
They also worked together on the track “The Birds Don’t Sing,” up for best rap song, and “Chains & Whips,” a best rap performance nominee.
Best New Artists
All of the best new artist nominees are performing at the 2026 Grammys. The list includes:
After getting her start on TikTok, Addison Rae released her self-titled debut album, “Addison,” in June 2025. The album includes hits like “Fame is a Gun” and “Diet Pepsi.”

Alex Warren began his career by sharing his music on YouTube and TikTok before his song “Ordinary” became a huge success last year. The song, which landed on top of the Billboard Hot 100 chart, was the lead single from his debut studio album, “You’ll Be Alright Kid.”

Girl group Katseye, with members Daniela Avanzini, Lara Raj, Manon Bannerman, Megan Skiendiel, Sophia Laforteza and Yoonchae Jeong, was formed on the 2023 reality competition show “Dream Academy.” The following year, the group released singles, “Debut” and “Touch,” and have skyrocketed since then. Their second EP, “Beautiful Chaos,” arrived in June 2025 and featured hits like “Gnarly” and “Gabriela.”

Leon Thomas, whose fans have followed his journey since his “Victorious” days, is already a Grammy winner thanks to SZA’s hit “Snooze,” which he co-wrote and won best R&B song at the 2024 Grammys. Now, he has received six Grammy nominations for his album “MUTT.” In addition to best new artist, he is also vying for best R&B album, best traditional R&B performance, best R&B song, best R&B performance and album of the year.

Oliva Dean has fans crooning along to her music all year long. The British breakout star achieved global success last year thanks to her earworms “Man I Need” and “So Easy (To Fall in Love).” Both songs are from her second album, “The Art of Loving,” which has steadily been climbing its way up the Billboard 200 chart. In November, Dean was the musical guest on “Saturday Night Live.”

Alternative artist Sombr, who is also a record producer, has an MTV Video Music Award and is looking to add a Grammy to his collection of trophies. Last year, he released his debut studio album, “I Barely Know Her,” with the hits “Back to Friends” and “Undressed.” The music video for “Back to Friends” earned him a VMA for best alternative video.

Bilingual pop band The Marías — lead singer María Zardoya, drummer Josh Conway, guitarist Jesse Perlman and keyboardist Edward James — has been making music for around a decade. But, their breakout moment came in 2022 when they were featured on Bad Bunny’s song “Otro Atardecer.” Then, their song “No One Noticed” from their second album, “Submarine,” went viral. In 2025, the group also worked with Selena Gomez and Benny Blanco on the song “Ojos Tristes” from the couple’s joint album.

British artist Lola Young, who recently took a break from performing to focus on her health, is the final best new artist nominee. Her song “Messy” from the 2024 album “This Wasn’t Meant for You Anyway” went viral on TikTok after years of making music. She performed the hit on “The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon” in January 2025. She was also featured on fellow 2026 Grammy nominee Tyler, the Creator’s song “Like Him” from his album “Chromakopia.”

title: Rob Gronkowski Relaxes His ‘Tight End’ for Prostate Cancer Screening Super Bowl Ad
summary: Super Bowl champions Bruce Arians and Rob Gronkowski star in a Super Bowl ad to encourage men to get screened for prostate cancer with an easy blood test.
story: Super Bowl champions Bruce Arians and Rob Gronkowski star in a cheeky Super Bowl ad from Novartis designed to encourage men to get screened for prostate cancer with an easy blood test.
Arians and Gronkowski appeared on TODAY Jan. 29 as paid spokespeople for the Novartis campaign to exclusively reveal the ad ahead of the Feb. 8 game.

The ad stars current NFL tight ends George Kittle (San Francisco 49ers) and Colby Parkinson (Los Angeles Rams) joining well-known retired tight ends Gronkowski (Tampa Bay Buccaneers), Greg Olsen (Carolina Panthers), Tony Gonzalez (Atlanta Falcons), Delanie Walker (Tennessee Titans) and Vernon Davis (San Francisco 49ers). The group of players relaxes with yoga, in a pool, painting and more while Enya’s “Only Time” plays underneath.
“You know what these tight ends are so relaxed about?” Arians says as he appears onscreen. “Prostate cancer screenings. They’ve learned there’s a simple, finger-free blood test.”

Arians, 73, who led the Tampa Bay Buccaneers to victory in Super Bowl 55 with Gronkowski at tight end, reminds viewers that roughly one in eight men will be diagnosed with prostate cancer in their lifetime, noting he is among that number. He had surgery for prostate cancer in 2007.
“I’m here today because I caught it early,” he continues.
Gronkowski, who won three other Super Bowls as a member of the New England Patriots, meanwhile, can be seen calmly brushing a horse.
“Gronk, what’d I teach you?” Arians asks.
“Relax your tight end,” Gronkowski replies before we see a shot of several posteriors loosening up as the clip comes to a (rear) end. “Prostate screenings starts with a simple blood test.”

Several medical groups recommend most men talk to their doctors about prostate screening beginning at age 45, although it could be 40 for those in high risk groups. Despite that, roughly 40.3 million men who are age-eligible have never had a prostate-specific antigen (PSA) blood test, according to a news release from Novartis.
Arians told TODAY he's "doing fantastic" and gets his PSA checked annually. Still, he believes that more men need to get checked.

"I think the stereotype of the finger test scares too many guys off," he said. "But when you look at early detection and saving lives, it's the best way to do it because this is a disease that's killing too many men that shouldn't be killing them."
Craig Melvin noted that Al Roker is a prostate cancer survivor as are some of his own relatives, before Gronkowski reiterated the importance of screening.
"Prostate cancer is one of the most common cancers among men in the U.S., and men fear to get screened because of the finger tests," he said.

"And we're here to raise awareness that it's just as simple as a blood test with Novartis here to get screened and see if you have prostate cancer or if it's developing," he added. "And you want to catch it early, as well, because if you catch it early, that's when it's most treatable."
For more screening information, the campaign encourages viewers to visit RelaxItsABloodTest.com.
